payload.answer: Store the 500-row CSV as immutable sources and build normalized, versioned memory cards (derived views and indexes) with explicit lineage. Do not alter or supersede the original source. Provide on-demand materialized views from the memory system.
payload.decision: Store by normalized, versioned memory cards with deduplicated entities and explicit lineage; preserve original data as immutable source cards; materialize derived views on demand

entity_resolution.reason: No preexisting durable entities referenced; treat as new, immutable source; avoid merging with other datasets without explicit provenance

memory_cards.statement: CSV table with 500 rows containing id, name, value repeated; treat as immutable source data
Create deduplicated, columnar-per-column storage for id, name, value with a unique row_id mapping to original row order; store as immutable, append-only segments
Build entity index for repeated names and ids to enable fast lookups; store as separate index segments with references to source rows

details: Memory cards created for source table, normalized view, and entity indexes
Provenance maintained; original source remains immutable

repair_options: Add explicit version tag to each memory_card and log provenance of derived views
Maintain append-only segments for all stored data
Preserve original CSV as a read-only root source with a stable identifier
Provide a data-diff mechanism to reconstruct history of changes if a row is updated in the source
